secret.description: The plaintext key — returned only once, at creation.

url.description: Adminer endpoint to POST the throwaway credential to.

action.description: Route pattern of the audited request.

detail.description: Canonical JSON detail; part of the hash chain.

broken_at_id.description: First tampered entry, when intact is false.

intact.description: False if the hash chain has been broken.

configured.description: False when hpd has no datastore configured, so sign-in is impossible until one is set.

needs_bootstrap.description: True on a fresh install with no admin yet.

serial.description: SOA serial, bumped on every change.

path.description: The absolute directory that was listed.
Path relative to the searched directory.
The absolute directory that was searched.

truncated.description: True when the result cap was hit.

host_key.description: Pinned SSH host key(s) (known_hosts format); when set, clones use strict host-key checking. ssh_key auth.

public_key.description: The generated ed25519 deploy key's public half (ssh_key auth).

webhook_url.description: Push webhook URL to register on the repository.

mfa_token.description: Short-lived token for the MFA step.

request_id.description: Correlates the response with server logs.

scope_note.description: Warns that toggling restarts FPM for every site on this version.

items.description: The php.ini keys the editor permits.

command.description: Start command, run as the site user in current/.

health_path.description: Optional; makes 'running' mean the app answers.

port.description: Local port the app listens on.

system_user.description: The dedicated non-root user the site runs as.

cpu_quota_pct.description: CPU quota as a percentage of one core (cgroup).

exists.description: False when the log file has not been created yet.

apiKey.description: Programmatic access with a scoped API key minted at POST /api/v1/account/api-keys. Sent as `Authorization: Bearer <key>`.

sessionCookie.description: Browser session established by POST /api/v1/auth/login. Mutations additionally require the double-submit CSRF token (cookie hp_csrf echoed in the X-CSRF-Token header) when CSRF is enabled.
sessionCookie.name: hp_session

info.description: Control-plane API for the HeroPanel hosting panel (hpd). All responses are wrapped in a `{data, meta}` envelope; errors in a `{error}` envelope. Authenticate with a session cookie (browser) or a bearer API key (programmatic).
info.title: HeroPanel API

200.description: Success.

items.description: Permission scopes to grant the key.

201.description: The secret is returned once, here only.

200.description: Key revoked.

site.description: Optional site uid to attribute it to.

values.description: Operator-supplied field values. Secret fields are ignored here and generated instead.

200.description: Generates any secret fields (never taken from input), checks memory feasibility, renders the template's compose file and brings it up. Returns the generated secrets **once** — they are not stored in a form the panel can return later, and are deliberately not written to the audit log.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Every deployable template, each with a memory-feasibility verdict against the host's available memory, so an app the host cannot run is marked before it is chosen.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Tears the app's stack down. Its volumes survive, so a redeploy reattaches the data.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: The app's running services.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Deletes the proxy site fronting the app, dropping its vhost. The app itself is left running on loopback — this takes down the front door, not the app.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

domain.description: The domain to serve the app on, e.g. blog.example.com.

200.description: Creates a proxy **site** whose vhost reverse-proxies to the app's live loopback port, resolved at render time so it follows a redeploy. `site.write`, not docker.write: it stands up a real site, with the domain/TLS/suspend controls every site has. The app keeps running on loopback; this is its front door. Refused with 409 if the app is already exposed, 404 if it is not deployed.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

post.summary: Expose an app on a domain

200.description: Whether the app is fronted by a domain, and if so which — plus the proxy site's uid and status. `{exposed:false}` when it is only reachable on loopback.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: A bounded tail of the app's combined logs. Force-audited: an app's logs carry its secrets and its users' data.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Success.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: 200 even when the chain is broken; check the intact field.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

201.description: Admin created; only allowed on a fresh install.

200.description: Session established, or MFA required.
Session cleared.
Success.
Success.
MFA disabled.
MFA enabled.

Success.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

host.description: Host the user may connect from.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: User dropped.

ns_ip.description: Glue A record for the primary NS.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission
Authenticated but missing the required permission

file.description: The compose file itself. Passed to docker on stdin, never a path.

site.description: Optional site uid to attribute the stack to.

200.description: The compose file is user-authored YAML and can request anything docker compose understands, so this is the module's explicit escape hatch: the panel labels and scopes the stack but does not harden arbitrary compose the way it hardens a container it builds. Every container the stack creates carries the managed label, so tear-down and the ownership boundary apply to it.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Removes the stack's containers and networks but never its volumes. Refused with 403 for a stack HeroPanel did not create.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: The services in a compose project and their state.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: A bounded tail of the whole stack's output. Force-audited. Query: tail.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Every container on the host, running or stopped, each flagged `managed` — whether HeroPanel created it. Unmanaged containers are listed (an admin must be able to see what is consuming the host) but cannot be modified. Query: site.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

command.description: Optional command. Everything after the image operand is the container's own argv, so a leading dash here is the program's flag, not docker's.

env.description: Environment variables. Sent to docker through stdin as an env-file, never as arguments — argv is world-readable via /proc, and this is where a generated password lives.

ports.description: Published ports as {host, container, proto}. Always bound to 127.0.0.1: docker's firewall rules are evaluated before the host's, so publishing on all interfaces would expose the container even on a host whose firewall denies the port.

site.description: Optional site uid to attribute the container to.

volumes.description: Mounts as {volume, path, read_only}. **Named volumes only** — a host path is not rejected but unrepresentable, which is what prevents `-v /:/host` and mounting the docker socket.

200.description: The container is created with HeroPanel's managed label and `no-new-privileges`. `--privileged`, `--cap-add`, `--device`, `--userns` and host namespaces have no corresponding field and cannot be produced.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Managed containers only. Never removes the container's volumes: deleting a container is routine, deleting its data is a separate and explicit act. Query: force.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Docker's own inspect payload, passed through unmodelled.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: WebSocket upgrade. Binary frames carry terminal bytes in both directions; JSON text frames carry resize/exit. `docker.write` rather than `docker.read`: a shell inside a container can stop the process, read its secrets and edit its data. Refused with 403 unless the container carries HeroPanel's managed label — a shell in someone else's container would bypass every other refusal in this module. Query: shell (/bin/sh, /bin/bash, /bin/ash), cols, rows.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

get.summary: Open a shell inside a container

200.description: A bounded tail (max 2000 lines), stdout and stderr separately. Force-audited: container logs routinely carry connection strings and customer data. Query: tail, timestamps.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: WebSocket upgrade. Binary frames carry log output as the container writes it; a JSON text frame carries the exit when the follow ends. The one-way, live twin of the polled logs read — same `docker.read` grant, same force-audit, because logs carry secrets. Not ownership-gated: reading any container's logs is allowed. Query: tail, timestamps.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Managed containers only.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Refused with 403 unless the container carries HeroPanel's managed label, enforced in the broker.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: One sample, not a stream — the client polls, so a wedged container can never hold a request open.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Sends SIGTERM with a 30s grace before SIGKILL. Managed containers only.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Images present on the host.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Reclaims disk from images no container needs. Dangling (untagged) layers only by default; query all=true also removes every image no container uses. Docker's own in-use check still protects anything running or stopped.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Fetches the image. A write, not a read: it places someone else's code on the host and consumes disk. Can take minutes.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Deletes an image by id or reference. Images carry no managed label, so ownership is not checked here — instead docker's refusal to remove an image a container still uses is passed straight through. Query: force (detaches extra tags only; does not override the in-use check).

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Whether a usable daemon is present and its version. An absent daemon is reported as available:false with the daemon's own reason — not an error, because "no Docker on this host" is a state the UI renders.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Every network on the host, each flagged `managed`.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

site.description: Optional site uid to attribute it to.

200.description: Always a bridge network: a container on the host network shares the host's stack outright, discarding the isolation that made containerising it worthwhile.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Managed networks only.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: The network's full record, including the containers connected to it (docker's inspect payload carries them). Read-only, so not ownership-guarded.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: One sample for all containers. Same measurement as the per-container route, so the two views cannot disagree.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Every volume on the host, each flagged `managed`.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

site.description: Optional site uid to attribute it to.

200.description: Creates a named volume carrying HeroPanel's managed label.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Deletes the volume **and its contents**. Refused with 403 for volumes HeroPanel did not create — this is the one operation in the module that destroys data, and an unmanaged volume usually belongs to a database.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: The volume's full record plus the containers that mount it — including unmanaged ones, so the destructive remove is an informed decision. Read-only, so not ownership-guarded.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Success.
Success.
Success.
Recent firings and resolutions, newest first. Query: limit (default 100, max 500).

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: The configured threshold rules. Notification targets are **never** returned — they are write-only, and a Telegram token is sealed at rest.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

for_sec.description: Seconds the breach must persist before firing (0 = immediate).

notify_target.description: Webhook URL, or Telegram bot token + chat id. Sealed at rest; requires a data key for the non-log kinds.

200.description: Creates a rule. It fires only after the breach has persisted for for_sec, so a one-tick spike pages nobody. webhook/telegram targets are sealed with the panel's data key (refused with 503 when none is configured).

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Removes a rule. Its past events are kept as history.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Toggles a rule without deleting it.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Node CPU / memory / load / disk over a bounded range (query range=1h|6h|24h|7d|30d, default 24h). A raw sample a minute is kept ~48h and folded into hourly averages kept ~30d, so the service returns raw within the window and hourly beyond it. Mounted only when a datastore is present.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: One snapshot of the host: CPU %, load, memory/swap, uptime and per-filesystem disk usage. A one-shot read for the initial paint — the live dashboard is pushed over the `monitor:node` WebSocket channel (subscription-gated, so an unwatched panel samples nothing) rather than polling this.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Whether the services the host depends on (web server, database, cache) are active, read through the broker's service.status capability. Live view: the `monitor:services` channel.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Each site's live memory, CPU % and task count, read from its cgroup v2 accounting (the slice every site runs in, with accounting on since it was created). A site whose slice has no cgroup yet reports `present:false`. Live view: the `monitor:sites` channel.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Success.
Success.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Success.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Success.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission
Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Site deleted.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Success.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: The site's backups (newest first), its schedule/retention policy, and which targets are configured. Every archive is zstd-compressed and sealed with chunked AES-256-GCM before it touches any target — a stolen disk or bucket yields ciphertext.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

level.description: full | incr; empty picks automatically (full for a fresh chain, incremental after).

200.description: Archives the site (GNU tar --listed-incremental, zstd), seals it, and stores it on the target. Requires a data key (HP_SECRET_KEY) — encrypted-at-rest is not optional. 503 without one.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

db_uid.description: Optional: a panel-managed database whose FULL dump rides along with every backup as a second sealed object (SQL dumps do not do incrementals). Empty = files only.

enabled.description: Whether the scheduler backs this site up.

keep_chains.description: How many full+incremental chains to retain (1–30).

200.description: The scheduler sweeps hourly and backs up any enabled site whose newest backup is older than its interval; a new full chain retires the oldest beyond keep_chains.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Removes the backup **and every later backup in its chain** — they depend on it, and saying so beats a chain that breaks silently at restore time. The database dump object, when the backup carried one, goes with it. Returns the removed uids.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

db_name.description: Optional: create a NEW database with this name and import the backup's database dump into it — the original database is never touched, for the same reason the tree goes into a new site.

200.description: Provisions a fresh site and replays the backup's chain into it (the full, then each incremental in order — deletions included). The original keeps serving untouched, so a mistaken restore destroys nothing; a backup that fails authentication is refused and nothing is written. Returns the new site (plus the new database when one was restored).

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

202.description: Clone started; copies the document root (not DB/git/runtime).

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: The site's cron jobs. Each is a real systemd timer triggering a oneshot service that runs as the site's user, in its home, inside its cgroup slice.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

command.description: The command, one line. Runs as the site user in the site home — never root.

200.description: Creates the job and enables its timer. Overlap policy comes free from systemd: a oneshot still running when its timer fires again is not started a second time, so a slow job never stacks. Persistent=true also runs a job missed during downtime once.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Disables the timer and removes the job.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Disabling removes the systemd timer (the definition is kept); enabling re-applies it.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: A bounded tail of the job's captured stdout+stderr (the launcher appends to a log in the site's logs directory, so this works without the journal). Force-audited.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Triggers the job immediately, so it can be tested without waiting for the timer.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Success.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission
Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Domain removed.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Query: path=<site-relative path>. Refuses the site root.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Query: path=<site-relative dir> (empty lists the site root). Baremetal sites only.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Streams a zip of the directory (application/zip). Query: path=<site-relative directory>; empty means the site root. The archive is built server-side and deleted once the response completes, so nothing is left in the site's tree.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Mode changed.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Ownership reset to the site's Linux user. The target account cannot be chosen — it is always the site's own user.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

items.description: Site-relative entries, all from the same folder.

200.description: Site walled off behind a 503 while keeping its domain mapping.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

101.description: Upgrades to a WebSocket carrying a PTY session run as the site's Linux user. Query: cwd (site-relative, clamped), cols, rows. Terminal bytes travel as binary frames in both directions; JSON text frames carry control messages ({type:"resize",cols,rows} in, {type:"exit",exit_code} / {type:"error",message} out). Force-audited: the session records the Linux account it ran as.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Recordings for this site, newest first. Query: limit, offset.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

get.summary: List a site's recorded terminal sessions

200.description: Success.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission
Authenticated but missing the required permission
Authenticated but missing the required permission
Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Sealed snapshots of the panel's own database (newest first) plus the active policy. Every snapshot is full and stands alone. Restore is deliberately out-of-band: `hpd decrypt` on the sealed object plus the documented manual steps — a panel that needs its database back cannot be trusted to serve that request.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Snapshots the panel's database (SQLite VACUUM INTO, or mysqldump via the broker), seals it, and stores it on the policy's target. Requires a data key (HP_SECRET_KEY).

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Removes one snapshot, row and stored object. No chains here — each snapshot stands alone.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Success.
All recordings, newest first. Query: limit, offset.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Removes the row and its file. Separate from the read permission on purpose: destroying an audit artifact is grantable to fewer people than viewing one.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Who opened the session, as which Linux user, how long it ran, and whether the recording is complete.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Streams the session as asciicast v2 (application/x-asciicast). Force-audited — this is the route that hands over the transcript. Input typed while the terminal had echo disabled (a password prompt) was redacted before it was ever written.

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required permission

200.description: Success.

200.description: The process is up.
